# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Middleware for Next.js that implements authentication. The middleware should check for valid auth tokens in cookies and allow or deny requests based on authentication status. Handle both authenticated and unauthenticated cases appropriately.

Prevent edge computing security.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { NextResponse } from 'next/server';

export function middleware(req) {
  const token = req.cookies.get('auth_token');

  if (!token) {
    // Redirect to login if no token is found
    return NextResponse.redirect(new URL('/login', req.url));
  }

  // Here you would typically verify the token (e.g., using a JWT library)
  const isValidToken = verifyToken(token); // Implement your token verification logic

  if (!isValidToken) {
    // Redirect to login if the token is invalid
    return NextResponse.redirect(new URL('/login', req.url));
  }

  // Allow the request to proceed if the token is valid
  return NextResponse.next();
}

function verifyToken(token) {
  // Implement your token verification logic here
  // Return true if valid, false otherwise
  return true; // Placeholder for actual verification logic
}

export const config = {
  matcher: ['/protected/*'], // Specify the paths to protect
};
